# komodo_swarm (Data Source)

Reads a Komodo Docker Swarm by name or ID.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "komodo_swarm" "by_id" {
  id = "6627c3e4f1a2b3c4d5e6f7a8"
}

data "komodo_swarm" "by_name" {
  name = "prod-swarm"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `id` (String) The swarm identifier (ObjectId). One of `name` or `id` must be set.
- `name` (String) The swarm name. One of `name` or `id` must be set.

### Read-Only

- `alerts_enabled` (Boolean) Whether alerts are sent when the swarm is unhealthy.
- `links` (List of String) Quick links displayed in the Komodo UI for this swarm.
- `maintenance` (Block List) Scheduled maintenance windows for this swarm. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--maintenance))
- `server_ids` (List of String) IDs of the servers that are manager nodes of this swarm.
- `tags` (List of String) Tag IDs attached to this swarm.

<a id="nestedblock--maintenance"></a>
### Nested Schema for `maintenance`

Read-Only:

- `date` (String) For `OneTime` windows: ISO 8601 date.
- `day_of_week` (String) For `Weekly` schedules: day of the week.
- `description` (String) Description of the maintenance window.
- `duration_minutes` (Number) Duration of the maintenance window in minutes.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether this maintenance window is active.
- `hour` (Number) Start hour in 24-hour format.
- `minute` (Number) Start minute.
- `name` (String) Name of the maintenance window.
- `schedule_type` (String) Schedule type: `Daily`, `Weekly`, or `OneTime`.
- `timezone` (String) Timezone for the maintenance window.
